dataset: Liverpool Cancer iChart Drug Interaction Database

pi_items.source: FDA approval records, NEJM publications, cancer.gov, Mayo Clinic drug database - Dabrafenib is a BRAF inhibitor approved by FDA in 2013 for treating BRAF-mutated melanoma
DrugBank, FDA drug database, WebMD, RxList - Carbamazepine is a potent antiepileptic drug (anticonvulsant) used to treat epilepsy and other conditions

pi_items.verified_value: Confirmed - Carbamazepine is a significant inducer of CYP3A4, and Dabrafenib is a CYP3A4 substrate. Carbamazepine increases Dabrafenib metabolism, decreasing its plasma levels. This is a clinically significant interaction requiring therapy modification.
pi_items.source: Drugs.com interaction checker, Mayo Clinic, Medscape, medical literature on CYP3A4 enzyme induction. FDA drug development guidance documents on enzyme inducers and substrates.

Task description explicitly mentions both pages: agent must tap Disclaimer button on Welcome screen (Part 1) and navigate through drug interaction search to reach Interaction Details page (Part 2). Cancer iChart app documentation confirms these page names.

pi_items.verified_value: Cancer iChart - A real, publicly available app developed by University of Liverpool and Radboud UMC, available on Google Play Store and Apple App Store
pi_items.source: Google Play Store listing, Apple App Store, University of Liverpool official news (June 2017), cancer-druginteractions.org official website, druginteractions.org

pi_items.metadata_value: App maintains offline drug interaction database
pi_items.verified_value: Cancer iChart is an offline-capable application with downloadable drug interaction data. Original website offline since September 2023, but app continues to function with cached/downloaded interaction data.
pi_items.source: University of Liverpool news and druginteractions.org status updates - original website ceased hosting in September 2023 due to lack of sustainable funding, but archived content available

privileged_info_summary: Dabrafenib is a verified BRAF inhibitor cancer drug approved by FDA in 2013 for BRAF-mutated melanoma treatment. Carbamazepine is a verified antiepileptic medication. These drugs have a verified clinically significant interaction where Carbamazepine induces CYP3A4 metabolism of Dabrafenib, requiring therapy modification. The task uses the verified Liverpool Cancer iChart application (University of Liverpool & Radboud UMC), a real, publicly available drug interaction checker app available on Google Play and Apple App Store. The Disclaimer and Interaction Details pages are verified components of the app workflow.
